# TradeRoot

> Performance coaching for prop firm and futures traders. We use clinical psychology methods to identify and interrupt the behavioral patterns that prevent skilled traders from executing consistently.

TradeRoot was founded by Aayush Namdev (funded prop trader, MA Psychology) and Khushi Narwal (MA Psychology, BSc Mathematics). We work with traders who already know how to trade but cannot execute what they know. The problem is not strategy. It is pattern.

## Who we help

- Prop firm traders who keep failing evaluations despite technical competence
- Futures traders who move stop losses, revenge trade, or cut winners early
- Traders who have tried mindset books, journaling, and discipline-based approaches without lasting change
- Anyone experiencing the gap between knowing and doing

## What we do

One-on-one performance coaching sessions: 60 minutes, video call, both founders present. Every session. The method has three stages: pattern recognition, origin tracing, and witnessed integration. Sessions are not therapy. We do not diagnose or treat clinical conditions. We use clinical methods applied to trading performance.

## Why it works

Behavioral loops in trading have origin points that are almost never about trading. Most trading psychology treats symptoms. We work at the source. The gap between knowing and doing is not a willpower problem. It is a pattern problem.

## Pricing

- Single session: $47 (early access, first 15 clients)
- Four-session series: $340
- Free 10-minute clarity call: https://cal.com/traderoot/free-10-min-call

## Contact

- Website: https://traderoot.space
- Email: hello@traderoot.space
- Free call: https://cal.com/traderoot/free-10-min-call

## Articles

- https://traderoot.space/articles/why-you-fight-the-market.html — Why traders personify the market and fight it after a loss; the survival circuit mechanism underneath revenge trading, why discipline never fixes it, and what root-cause work looks like instead
- https://traderoot.space/articles/why-prop-traders-fail.html — Why 93% of prop firm traders never receive a payout; the failure mode is almost never technical, it is behavioral
- https://traderoot.space/articles/prop-firm-challenge-discipline.html — How the structure of every prop firm challenge (the simulated number, the target math, the loop cadence) pushes disciplined traders toward reckless sizing
- https://traderoot.space/articles/trading-frequency-problem.html — Why trading is not a discipline problem but a frequency problem; the nervous system gets calibrated to a signal nothing else matches, and rule breaking carries its own reward
- https://traderoot.space/articles/the-loneliness-of-trading.html — The social cost of trading: withholding your edge from close friends, the timeline mismatch with family, and why payout is the only language that translates
